title: daphne cneorum subsp. arbusculoides
intro: Daphne cneorum subsp. arbusculoides, also known as dwarf pink daphne, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Thymelaeaceae. It is a small, shrubby perennial native to the mountains of southwestern Europe and western North Africa. The plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory compounds, which are effective in treating wounds and reducing inflammation. Daphne cneorum subsp. arbusculoides can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are said to have various health benefits, including soothing digestive issues and relieving respiratory problems.
